revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]]、多胡若宮命…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]]、多胡若宮命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子（たご）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（[[1881年]]）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]] * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]]、多胡若宮命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子（たご）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（[[1881年]]）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]] * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

[[JA:哆胡神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q134893138}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|哆胡神社|version=103480985|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]]、多胡若宮命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子（たご）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（[[1881年]]）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]] * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

==End Japanese==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tago Shrine | native_name = 哆胡神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Tago Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.806188|138.764678}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|fa|کوتوشیرونوشی|fr|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|ko|고토시로누시|ru|Котосиронуси|simple|Kotoshironushi|th|โคโตชิโรนูชิ|zh|事代主神|WD=Q8009611}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[積羽八重事代主命]]、多胡若宮命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]田子（たご）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（[[1881年]]）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]] * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（[[1881年]]）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[積羽八重事代主命]] * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{translated page|ja|哆胡神社|version=103480985|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 哆胡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子1103-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}、多胡若宮命 |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社}}、{{jalink|式内社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子（たご）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}} * 多胡若宮命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{nihongo|'''Tago Shrine'''|哆胡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 哆胡神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子1103-1
|位置   = {{coord|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}、多胡若宮命
|社格   = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 11月2日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=哆胡神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|48|22.5|N|138|45|52.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子（たご）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
* 多胡若宮命

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}}

'''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子（たご）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
* 多胡若宮命

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}}

'''哆胡神社'''（たごじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}田子（たご）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
田子地区の中央、大田子海岸の南方脇の高台（合ノ浦）上に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である哆胡神社（たごじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 多胡の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

元々は北部の大田子地区に八幡神社として鎮座していたが、明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に南部の井田子地区の三島神社と合祀して、現在地に遷座した<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
* 多胡若宮命

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - 静岡県神社庁

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Tago, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{jalink|Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Tago, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{jalink|Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}}

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Tago, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{jalink|Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

{{DEFAULTSORT:たこしんしや}}

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Tago, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Tago, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Tago, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Tago, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

'''Tago Shrine''' (たごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Tago, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Tago district, on high ground (Ainoura) just south of Ōtago Beach.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401179 哆胡神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Tago Shrine (たごじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Tago no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tago_title.htm 哆胡神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140309-01.html 多胡神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In 1873 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Originally it stood in the northern Ōtago district as Hachiman Shrine, but in 1881 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}, Meiji 14) it was merged with Mishima Shrine of the southern Itago district and moved to its present location<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumiha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
* Tago Wakamiya-no-Mikoto
